checks.a_analysis: The output contains text that is directly derived from the source slides. No invented paragraphs, section headings like 'Slide 1', or introductory text were found that were not based on the source PDF. Normalizations like line breaks in the title page are acceptable.

The metadata correctly identifies 'Doug Newcomb' as the author. No phone numbers, emails, or URLs were found in the provided JSON sections that were not present in the source. Note: Page 7 of the source contains a URL (http://arcg.is/2kqc0h9) but this page is missing from the output entirely, so no fabrication occurred in the surviving text.

The source PDF consists of 14 distinct pages/slides. The output JSON explicitly contains a placeholder stating '7 sections omitted for token budget'. Consequently, the content from pages 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, and 10 is entirely absent from the output. This includes the goals slide, operations overviews, ground truth locations, planning maps, and survey activity slides.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains 14 pages, including slides titled 'National Coastal Mapping Program Goals' (p4), '2016 Post-Matthew Operations Overview' (p5), 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' (p6), 'Post-Matthew Data Production Status Map' (p7), '2017 NCMP Planning' (p9), and 'NOAA Channel survey activities' (p10).

checks.a_analysis: The source document contains at least three tables: a large 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations' table on page 6 (~23 rows), a 'Flight Block Status' table on page 7, and a data table on page 8. The output JSON contains zero tables, resulting in a 100% loss of tabular data rows from the source.
checks.b_evidence_source: Table on Page 6 with columns Longitude, Latitude, Location, State and approximately 23 rows of data. Table on Page 7 (Flight Block Status) and Page 8 (Elevation Difference).
checks.b_evidence_location: Metadata and throughout sections (specifically missing from omitted pages 6, 7, 8)

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: For the sections that were successfully converted (Pages 1, 2, 3, 11, 12, 13, 14), the text content accurately reflects the source. For example, the list of hurricane activities on page 2 is fully captured in the unordered list items.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text extracted from the included pages matches the source exactly. Line breaks and list formatting are maintained. No semantic changes or incorrect data values were identified in the processed text.

checks.a_analysis: The output was examined for any instances of text or sections being repeated. Each slide's content appears exactly once in the corresponding section of the JSON. No duplicated paragraphs or lists were found.

The heading hierarchy is generally consistent with the document's structure as a presentation. However, in the final section, the title of Page 14 ('Hurricane Matthew Request - Landsat') is included as a heading element with 'level': 2 inside the element list of Section 12. Since the section title itself acts as a major header (H2 level), nesting a sibling-level heading (level 2) within its elements creates an incorrect hierarchy. Page 14 represents a distinct topic and should have been rendered as a separate section to maintain a proper H1-H2 structure.
checks.b_evidence_output: {"title": "Hatteras Inlet Satellite-Derived Bathy", "elements": [ ... { "type": "heading", "level": 2, "text": "Hurricane Matthew Request - Landsat" }, ... ]}

checks.a_analysis: The provided JSON sections do not contain any table structures. The tables present in the source PDF (e.g., on pages 6, 7, and 8) are located in sections that were omitted from the provided JSON snippet for token budget reasons. Within the visible sections, no tables are present or corrupted.

The reading order of the output follows the sequential order of the slides in the source PDF. Although Page 14 is merged into the section containing Pages 12 and 13, the content within that section follows the correct sequence (P12 -> P13 -> P14). The information flow remains logical.
The list on Page 2 is correctly identified as an unordered list with seven items, accurately reflecting the source text and formatting. Other lists in the document occur on omitted pages.

The visible sections of the output do not contain any links. While the source PDF contains URLs on pages 7 and 9, those pages are omitted from the provided JSON. No fabricated or broken links are observed in the presented content.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML document correctly maintains the linear organization of the slide presentation. Each slide's content is grouped under appropriate headings that match the slide titles. The transition from a fixed-layout slide deck to a responsive single-column HTML document is handled logically, allowing the user to find information in the same sequence as the original. Normal differences in font and spacing are expected and do not disrupt the flow of information.

There is a significant table on page 6 ('Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations') and a small data table on page 8. Both are missing from the HTML output. However, as they appear to be part of complex graphical slides and are replaced by image placeholders (green boxes), this defect is captured under Check V3 (Images missing or broken) to avoid double-counting. No other standalone tables are present in the source.

Virtually all meaningful graphical content from the source PDF is missing or failed to render in the HTML output, replaced by solid green or patterned placeholder boxes. This includes critical maps showing hurricane operations (Page 5), ground truth locations and data tables (Page 6), data production status (Page 7), complex data visualizations/charts (Page 8), planning maps (Page 9), and satellite imagery/bathymetry charts (Pages 11-14). These images are essential to the document's purpose of communicating coordination and mapping results.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 5: Map of US East Coast showing operations; Page 6: Map and table of ground truth locations; Page 8: 'Elevation Difference and Volume' map/chart; Pages 11-14: Hurricane Matthew imagery and bathymetry maps.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows solid green boxes and a pixelated/camo box in place of these images under headings like '2016 Post-Matthew Operations Overview', 'Post-Matthew Ground Truth Locations', 'Data Visualization', and 'Hatteras Inlet Satellite-Derived Bathy'.
checks.b_evidence_location: HTML sections corresponding to PDF pages 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 11, 12, 13, and 14.

checks.a_analysis: A review of the HTML output shows no instances of repeated text blocks, tables, or images. Each section corresponds uniquely to a slide from the source PDF, and no content is erroneously doubled.
